Key.Original: Select skills and assign active abilities
Unlock this skill/tier by spending enough points in the previous tiers
Click here to choose an ability

You currently have no skills unlocked in this category.

New skills can be unlocked via the skill tree.
Details of the selected ability will be shown here.
This skill has been temporarily disabled so that it can be improved.
You already have advanced training in another area.
You already have another weapon specialisation.
The photo does not contain the site
The site is barely visible in the photo
The photo shows some of the site
The photo shows enough of the site
The photo shows most of the site

Unlocks weapons and equipment suitable for a rifleman when you level up.\nRiflemen are well-suited to mid-range and precision engagements, having access to a selection of rifles and some scopes.\nNew gear is unlocked every other level.

Unlocks weapons and equipment suitable for a scout when you level up.\nScouts prioritise quiet, close-range engagements, having access to a selection of suppressed SMGs.\nNew gear is unlocked every other level.

Unlocks weapons and equipment suitable for a marksman when you level up.\nMarksmen excel at mid and long range, with access to precise, semi-automatic weapons and a range of scopes.\nNew gear is unlocked every other level.

Unlocks weapons and equipment suitable for a grenadier when you level up.\nGrenadiers can deal with groups of NVA quickly, having access to a variety of weapon types and grenade launcher attachments.\nNew gear is unlocked every other level.

Unlocks weapons and equipment suitable for a machinegunner when you level up.\nMachinegunners can easily suppress and subdue the NVA, with access to a range to light machineguns.\nNew gear is unlocked every other level.
Unlocks basic pistols and melee weapons, proximity claymores, M67 grenades, and NVG binoculars
Unlocks better pistols and melee weapons, timed claymores, and V40 grenades
Unlocks the sawn-off M79 grenade launcher, WP proximity claymores, and bayonets
Unlocks enemy sidearms and melee weapons
Unlocks WP, CS gas and incendiary grenades.
The first time you run out of ammo for your primary weapon, receive bonus magazines
Maintaining automatic fire will slowly reduce your recoil (up to 50%). This reduction is reset when you release the trigger or reload.
For the next 120 seconds, you have 50% chance to not consume ammo and you suppress enemies 100% more.
For the next 60 seconds, your weapon sway is reduced by 100%
Canteens restore stamina. Machetes clear brush.
50% less weapon sway when crouched or prone
Firing your weapon does not break Stop, Focus
Carry 30% more weight and consume 20% less stamina
Suppressed enemies will be marked for you. This mark disappears when their suppression returns to zero naturally.
You do not consume grenades when throwing/firing them
When you are detected, stamina consumption is reduced by 30% for the next 30 seconds.
Use concussion grenades to stun enemies outside lethal range and capture POWs for bonus rewards.

Gain advanced training as a pointman.
You can only have one advanced training.
Unlocks the trapkit for detecting traps.
Reduced skill costs in the pointman tree.
Enables encountering an ammo dump where booby-trapped ammo can be planted for bonus rewards.
Speed up the activation time of “Stop, Focus” by 50%
When moving by yourself, trackers are less likely to search for you
Gain one extra task hint on the map at the beginning of a mission
Melee kills will not raise alertness
When looking through binoculars, pressing 'Reveal Target' gives an approximate distance
Inspected clues are marked on the map for the team
Inspecting a trail reveals the direction to the nearest site within 300m
Attracts enemies to a position 20m in the direction you’re facing
Inspects the nearest glint in the direction you're facing, up to 50m away
Trailing an enemy squad may reveal site information
Wearing enemy helmets increases the time it takes for them to spot you (unlocks recon pith helmet)
Range of "Stop, Focus" is extended by 100%
Enemy won’t detect you as long as you're undetected, walk and wear an enemy helmet.
Move freely while using Stop, Focus
Briefly mark all enemies within 150m in 3D and on the map
Throw knives for a silent ranged kill

Gain advanced training as a team leader.
You can only have one advanced training.
Unlocks a placeable ammo can with 1 use per team member.
Reduced skill costs in the team leader tree.
Unlocks a 414 wiretap kit that can intercept and record enemy communications for bonus rewards.
The map reveals some potential sources of water.
Enables marking a location for other team mates in the HUD.
Shows team members ammo state on the HUD.
If no teams are deployed, call a vote on time-of-day (all players):\n- DAY(0800)\n- NIGHT(1800)
All team members show up on each team member's map.
All team members consume no ammo.
During mission creation, reveal potential side-missions (e.g. wiretap).
Request covey give you the azimuth and distance to your nearest LZ, and adds a marker on the map.
Team members without rucksacks don't consume stamina.
Call other Team Leaders for a limited Remain Overnight vote.
Briefly distracts the enemy from all team members.
Gives the team: 50% reduced stamina consumption, hit shrug, 10% faster actions.
Team members are visible on your map.
After calling for extraction, request an alternate LZ from Covey (max 3 times).
Reduces the cooldown time of all ally abilities by 20% (maximum 35%).
Resets all team members' ability cooldowns.
A random aircraft arrives on station immediately with 480 seconds loiter time. Call airstrikes using your handheld radio.
The map reveals location hints for potential side-missions.
Enables setting a nearby team rally point on the map. Team members engaging in a desperate escape start within 250m of it.

Gain advanced training as a radio telephone operator.
You can only have one advanced training.
Unlocks radio backpacks and the ability to call in air support.
Reduced skill costs in the RTO tree.
Unlocks limited air support using the handheld radio with 50% longer arrival time, 50% slower strike time.
Reduces air support penalties from night by 75%.
Covey advises the team on enemy intensity and location of nearest forces.
While active, an AC-119K works your perimeter (1 use).
Reduces air support penalties from bad weather by 75%.
The current aircraft arrives immediately, and has its loiter time extended by 50%.
While active an Arclight is available (1 use).
Covey repeats your last airstrike automatically after 30 secs.
While active a BLU-82 is available, which will add an LZ where it lands. (1 use).

Gain advanced training as a medic.
You can only have one advanced training.
Unlocks reusable medical kits.
Reduced skill costs in the medic tree.
Enables encountering high value enemies that can be killed or captured for bonus rewards.
When the last first aid kit is used, gain 2 more.
Instantly stops bleeding on a player. Any new injuries will restart the bleeding.
Temporarily disable all injury effects on the team.
Enable sprinting while carrying a downed teammate.
Heals up to 2 wounds for each interaction.
Chance to learn additional info from dying enemies.
Reduces the effects of limb injuries on yourself.
Completely heal the person you're looking at.
Instantly take down an enemy soldier in melee range. They are then a POW and can be carried to the helicopter for bonus rewards.
Immediately get back up when the whole team is downed. Once per mission.

Gain advanced training as a tailgunner.
You can only have one advanced training.
Unlocks the toolkit and trapkit to detect and disarm traps. Unlocks standard claymores.
Reduced skill costs in the tail tree.
Enables encountering high-value enemy assets and infrastructure that can be destroyed for bonus rewards.
Automatically mark traps detected by your trapkit (within 4m).
When moving by yourself, your trail is less likely to be discovered.
The first time you run out of M14 "toe-popper" mines, receive 6 more M14 mines.
Rearm a recoilless rifle in your backpack.
Automatically place M14 toe-poppers behind you every 5m.
Unlocks enemy mines and quiet punji traps.
Loaded for elephant: When you consume your last rocket, receive another (max 2 uses).
Unlocks Mk II Trapkit with increased mine detection range (within 8m).
Use 75% of your ruck to carry a recoilless rifle with a single magazine (self assembly, 8 rounds HE).
4th July: You do not consume rockets when firing them.
Places 7 remote claymores in front of you.
Unlock advanced mines, explosives and throwable satchels.
Adds an action to cycle carried mines between proximity detonation, remote detonation and a timer.
Your placed explosives increase enemy alertness by a smaller amount.
Automatically place a selection of timed 40s claymores behind you every 15m
Craft punji traps and tripwires in the field.
Use silent sabotage actions on enemy infrastructure for reduced alertness and bonus rewards.
Reduce the cooldown time of all ally abilities by 20% (Global cap at 50%)
Makes intel glints appear 30% more frequently
When looking through binoculars, you can press 'Reveal Target' to get an approximate distance to the target
Briefly add a 3D marker to all enemies in within 150m, and add an approximate marker of their locations to your map.
For 60 seconds, enemies are much less likely to see you and hear you.
Team members are visible on the map
You have a 30% chance to not consume a utility item when using one.
Add 60 seconds to a player's bleedout timer.
For 30 seconds, the team has: 50% reduced stamina consumption, 40% hit shrug chance, all actions are 10% faster
Couldn't create a new mission: all target boxes are currently in use
Couldn't reserve target box %1 for mission - all target boxes are occupied
Mission creation failed for unexpected reason %1
No radio or RTO nearby to call for extraction.
No radio or RTO nearby, board the helicopter to force extraction.
Start extraction countdown? Team will be notified and have 30 seconds to board the helicopter.
Extract immediately? Remaining team members will be abandoned.

Key.English: Stay alive and wait for extraction.

Key.Original: %1 has added a photo to site %2.
Mark site location on the map

Key.English:
Scout the area for enemy sites and note them in your "Scouting Report".

HQ suggests checking following grids:
%1

%2
An enemy site is near this location.

Key.Original: Welcome to the %3beta%4 version of %3%11%4, a dynamic recon experience for individuals and teams of up to 6 players.%1%1Inspired by the namesake book from RT Idaho One-Zero and SOG Prairie Fire adviser John “Tilt” Stryker Meyer, in %3%11%4 you'll explore hostile NVA territory to investigate and locate enemy sites, before you try to extract safely and prepare for the next mission.%1%1As you gain experience fighting the NVA, you'll develop your skills and abilities while gaining access to new weapons and equipment.%1%1Before playing, %3it's strongly recommended you read 'Getting started'%4 in the field manual.%1%1The mission is currently in %3open beta%4 and not fully complete, with both bugs and unreleased features.%1%1However, based on the feedback from our Patrons and regular playtesters, we feel it's ready to share with the community, to get your feedback and give you the opportunity to enjoy what we've developed so far.%1%1As we progress from onwards to a full release, we'd love for you to %3share your thoughts in Discord%4, and help us collaboratively develop the game mode.%1%1The %3beta%4 version of %3%11%4 has most of the game mode's core mission gameplay in place, but other aspects of the game mode are still in development. Our immediate feature roadmap includes:%1%2 Releasing the Team Leader, Medic, RTO and Tail skill trees%1%2 Adding a new RTO system better suited to the game%1%2 Expanding points of interest with new interactions%1%2 Many quality of life improvements (e.g. improved mission UIs)%1%1%3%11%4 is currently designed for 1 to 6 players per mission, with up to 4 simultaneous missions running on a server.%1Please try playing solo, duo and in larger groups, so we can build a game mode everyone can enjoy!%1%1Thanks for reading, and enjoy the game!%1> The team at Savage Game Design%1%1%3Important links:%4%1%2 Discord%1%2 Online guide%1%2 %11 on the Steam Workshop%1%2 Source code
Thanks for helping us preserve S.O.G history through your Patreon support!

%3%11%4 brings together role-playing elements and player specialisations to create a thrilling behind enemy lines recon gamemode.%1You will be able to develop your custom character class and enlist for missions in the Prairie Fire, Daniel Boone and Nickel Steel AO's.%1Completing missions earns you XP and enables you to grow your abilities and mature into a MACV SOG 1-0.%1%1%2 Start by going into the nearby Tactical Operation Center (TOC)%1%2 Grab your favorite gear from the %3Armory%4%1%2 Adjust your skills at the %3Radio%4%1%2 Create or join a mission from the %3Tactical map%4%1%2 Once your team is ready, the leader can start the mission from the %3Tactical map%4
To get started, head into the nearby TOC and look around.

Running recon missions is the core element of the %3%11%4 gamemode.%1%1Once you and your team is ready to go on a mission, go to the %3tactical map%4, %3create a new mission%4 and select your area of operations (WIP).%1When the team has %3joined the mission%4, geared up and mentally prepared themselves, %3start the mission%4.%1%1You'll be inserted deep behind enemy lines, tasked with performing recon and %3scouting%4 multiple enemy sites.%1%1You can call for extraction at any time. If you make it out alive, the whole team will be awarded experience based on your team's performance and scouted sites.%1%1%3If nobody on your team survives, you will not receive any experience or rewards for your mission.%4
Now you've customised your gear, create or join a mission using the %3tactical map board%4.
You are now leading a mission. Other players will be able to join your mission until you depart. Make sure everyone is ready first!
You've joined another player's mission. The leader can start the mission at any time. You can leave the mission using the actions on the map board.
As the mission leader, you can call for extraction at any time.%1This will request a helicopter that will land at a nearby LZ. The mission will end once all non-incapacitated players are on-board.

Skills are grouped into four main archetypes, %3Rifleman%4, %3Recon%4, %3Fire Support%4 and %3Support%4.%1You can mix skills from all archetypes, creating your own specialized MACV-SOG character.%1%1You can access %3Skills menu%4 at the %3Radio%4. To learn skills you need %3Skill Points (SP)%4, which are gained by leveling up. To level up, you need to gather experience by running missions.%1%1There's two types of skills:%1%2 Passives - have passive effect on your character, affecting you all the time, do not need an activation%1%2 Abilities - need to be equipped in %3Abilities%4 menu at the %3Radio%4, they have short term effects once activated via %3Skill Wheel%4. There's two subtypes of abilities:%1 %2 Standard Abilities - offer a small benefit and are intended to be used frequently throughout the mission%1 %2 Ultimate Abilities - offer a large benefit and are intended to be used at critical moments during the mission%1%1<t size="1.25">Skill wheel</t>%1To use one of the skills assigned in the %3Abilities%4 menu, press %3`%4 (Grave / Backtick / Backquote) and select it from the %3Skill wheel%4
Recon teams were skilled warriors, training for the next mission.
You just levelled up and gained a skill point. Use them to learn new skills at the radio in the TOC.
You just acquired an active skill! Before you can use it, you need to equip it in the skill assignment menu at the radio.

%3%11%4 introduces simple yet impactful medical system featuring:%1%2 Increased player durability%1%2 Location-aware injuries that impact gameplay%1%2 Slow bleed-out, if left untreated%1%1<t size="1.25">Medical menu</t>%1%3Medical menu%4 allows treatment of the injuries suffered by player characters.%1To treat an injury you need to select an injured body part from the character %3silhouette%4 and apply %3medication%4 on it.%1Press %3H%4 to open medical menu of the person you're looking at, if there's no person under your cursor the menu of your character will open.%1Alternatively press %3Shift + H%4 to open the medical menu of your character.%1%1<t size="1.1">Medication</t>%1Available medication items:%1%2 First Aid Kit - Heals single wound level, single use, can be used by anybody%1%2 Medikit - Heals single wound level, infinite uses, can be used by players with "%14" skill from the %3Support%4 tree%1%1<t size="1.1">Debuffs</t>%1Character injuries will affect your performance, the more severe injuries the stronger debuffs will be applied. Type of debuffs depend on the injured body parts.%1Here are some example debuffs:%1%2 Head - increasingly blurred vision%1%2 Torso - reduced stamina%1%2 Arms - reduced accuracy and recoil control%1%2 Legs - reduced movement speed%1Multiple untreaded wounds will cause %3bleeding%4 which if not stopped will cause the character to fall %3unconscious%4.%1%1<t size="1.1">Unconsciousnes</t>%1After receiving critical amount of damage to vital body parts (head, torso) or bleeding out, the player character will fall %3unconscious%4, characters in this state need help of other players to wake up.%1If the player will be abandoned and left alone by other players, he'll %3respawn%4.%1Once all players in the mission are %3unconscious%4, the mission fails.
Bullets. Shrapnel. Recon teams rarely made it out unscathed.

Having the right equipment is essential to both surviving and succeeding as a recon team member.%1%1Currently, %3%11%4 uses the standard Arma 3 Arsenal to manage your gear. You can find these in the TOC building, at the main base.%1%1You'll also start the game with a typical recon team loadout, suitable for running a mission straight away.%1%1<t size="1.1">Key equipment</t>%1%1Certain pieces of equipment are particularly helpful in %3%11%4:%1%2 %3Cameras%4 can be used to photograph enemy sites. See %3Scouting%4 for more information.%1%2 %3Silenced weapons%4 make it easier to eliminate NVA forces without being noticed. However, they will still attract some attention!%1%2 %3Radio backpacks%4 can be used to call in air support, if you have the right %3Skills%4.%1%1<t size="1.1">Unlocking equipment</t>%1%1New equipment can be unlocked by unlocking the right %3Skills%4. All skills that unlock equipment have %3Equipment%4 in their name.
You can change your equipment using the nearby ammo boxes. See the field manual for recommended key equipment.

Scouting missions require players to survey the AO for enemy installations, mark them in their %3Scouting report%4 and extract.%1%1Mission overview%1You'll be inserted deep behind enemy lines, where you'll be outnumbered. You should avoid enemy contact as much as possible to avoid being overwhelmed.%1Move slowly, %3Stop and Focus%4 frequently to spot any threats before they spot you, gather as much intel as you can and extract alive.%1If the whole team is killed or incapacitated, all intel will be lost and no experience will be awarded.%1%1Scouting report%1%3Scouting report%4 is available in your map view, it is shared between your whole team.%1%1Filling in a new report entry:%1%2 Click %3Add item%4 button: a new row will be added%1%2 Select site type with button%1%2 Mark the site position with %3Mark site%4 button%1%1Photo camera%1You can photograph a site to gain experience for its data. After photographing a site, the map screen with the report will be shown.%1Assign the photo to a site with the button.
Area recon was a common task, with recon teams hunting for enemy fortifications or units.
The main goal of a recon mission is to locate enemy sites, and mark them in this notebook. Use a camera to capture photos of the sites.

Occasionally, you'll see a glint reflecting off of an object lying on the ground.%1These clues are often found near enemy sites, and can help you locate enemy positions.%1Interacting with a clue will give you an approximate bearing to an enemy site.%1%1Different enemy sites sometimes have different clues (WIP), so taking the time to learn the different clue types%1can help you be a more effective tracker.
Recon teams frequently deployed into the jungle with only a rough idea of what they might find.
You just saw light glinting off a nearby object! Find and inspect the object to help locate nearby sites.

You can stop and focus on your surroundings, to help you locate enemy activities.%1%1Nearby sounds from enemies such as footsteps, gunshots and speech will be shown visually, until you move or stop focusing.%1%1Additionally, when you're focusing, you'll more frequently see the light glinting off of clues. See %3Clues%4 for more information.%1%1It's strongly recommended that you stop and focus frequently, to prevent your team getting caught unawares.
You just focused on your surroundings. While focusing, you can visualise the sounds of nearby enemies and spot hints more quickly. Moving will exit this state.
The tactical map board allows you to create, join and leave missions.

When you're running a mission, many actions you take will alert the enemy that there's a S.O.G recon team operating in their area.%1%1As the enemy forces become more aware of your presence, they'll start %3actively hunting your team%4, and %3respond quicker to your activities%4.%1%1This means you should be careful when doing anything loud, or that might draw attention to you. %3Gunshots and explosions are a surefire way to compromise your mission%4.%1%1And remember, %3a suppressed weapon is not a silent weapon%4.
Gunshots, explosions and other actions you take on a mission will alert the enemy to your presence, increasing the threat to your team.

When deployed on a mission, you and your team will be outnumbered and likely outgunned.%1%1%3%11%4 adds a unique stealth system to help you avoid enemies and break contact if neccessary.%1%1Enemies will notice you based on:%1%2 How much of you they can see (consider your stance and cover)%1%2 How fast you're moving%1%2 How directly they're looking at you%1%2 How illuminated you are%1%2 How often you're engaging with the enemy (gunshots, grenades, etc)%1%1Here are some tips that will help you move around the jungle effectively and quietly:%1%2 Use cover as much as possible%1%2 If you need to sneak a look at the enemy, show as little of yourself as possible%1%2 Avoid moving when enemies are looking directly at you%1%2 Avoid flares, fires or any sources of man-made light at night%1%2 Avoid shooting or grenades until you're ready to be detected%1%2 If the enemy engages you, evade them for a while to break contact. Be aware that trackers may still hunt you!
Recon teams were heavily outnumbered by the NVA, and tried to avoid contact when possible.
Avoid enemy contact by hiding in bushes and behind trees, moving slowly and staying out of their line of sight.

When you're incapacitated due to your injuries, you've got two options available to you:%1%2 Wait for help from your team%1%2 Perform a desperate escape%1%1You can only make a desperate escape %3once per mission%4, or %3twice if you're playing alone%4.%1%1If you attempt a desperate escape, you'll scramble to get away from the enemy and %3reappear at a safe area nearby fully recovered%4.%1%1In your haste, you'll %3lose your backpack and several consumable items%4 in the process.
Recon teams would ditch their rucks in desperate circumstances so they could move faster.
When downed, once per mission you can conduct a desperate escape to get to safety. You'll lose your backback and some other items.

%3ADDED:%4%1%2 Added zombie gamemode variant (requires [Zombies and Demons - S.O.G. Prairie Fire compatibility mod])%1%1%3CHANGED:%4%1%2 Reworked the grenadier and scout weapon specialisms to better align with other trees%1%2 Reduced noise radii for gunshots%1%2 Rebalanced noise radii and alertness for explosions, to better align with the size of the "bang"%1%2 Overhauled enemy reinforcement scaling to avoid rapid, deadly escalation%1%1%3FIXED:%4%1%2 Fixed skill and level progress being lost in some instances when using profile storage (default)%1%2 Fixed skills being unlearnable in some instances when LAN hosting%1%2 Fixed several items being unavailable in the Arsenal%1%2 Fixed TFAR radios being filtered out by the Arsenal%1%2 Fixed skill wheel keybinding showing as 'anyanyany' in the Field Manual%1%2 Rewords 'Taking Notes' skill description - Thanks @FateTheTitan%1%2 Players no longer turn into AI on disconnect%1%2 Deletes helicopter on mission failure%1%2 Made the extraction helicopter land more intelligently, avoiding it being too high or low%1%2 Fixed multiple issues with broken equipment unlocks%1%2 Fixed reinforcements double-scaling with player count%1%2 Fixed site locations on Dong Giang, The Bra and Khe Sanh%1%2 Fixed level info not being show when the player first joins%1%2 Fixed players having 4 skill points (instead of 3) when they first join%1%2 Prevents mission creation falsely failing with "Zone reservation failed" message
Despite being injured, you manage to evade capture
Some of your supplies and equipment were lost in your fight to escape:
You'll respawn when unconscious and abandoned by the team.
The following items are not available to you, and have been removed:
Use external database? (Dedicated server + mod only)
Maximum number of players that can join a mission
Enables an object near the TOC allowing players to skip to level 5
